2. 
3. ## Model Router Skill
4. 
5. Use the `model-router` skill to keep model selection cost-aware and consistent with this repository's routing policy.
6. 
7. The model-router skill is designed to optimize model selection across all work types:
8. - For cost-effective execution, always prefer cheaper models for lightweight tasks
9. - For implementation, use balanced builder models
10. - For planning, debugging, or explicitly requested code review, escalate to stronger reasoning models
11. - For explicitly requested review work, prefer a different model than the one used for implementation to ensure diversity of thought
12. - Do not perform or imply an automatic post-implementation review unless the user explicitly asks for review or audit
13. 
14. **When to activate this skill:**
15. - When the user asks about model choice, cost, token usage, or pricing
16. - When the user explicitly asks for code review, audit, PR review, or approval readiness
17. - When the user wants to implement something and mentions cost, speed, or efficiency
18. - When the user is planning architecture or debugging a hard problem
19. 
20. If the repo-local `model-router` extension is active, it will:
21. 1. Classify each user prompt to detect the task type (lightweight, implementation, planning, debugging, review)
22. 2. Automatically switch the session model to the best fit
23. 3. Prefer a different model for review than was used for implementation, but only for explicit review requests
24. 4. Inject routing context so you understand why a model was chosen
25. 
26. If the extension is not active, follow the same routing policy manually.
27.

1. # Eval Harness CI
2. #
3. # Runs the model-router evaluation suite in CI, comparing router-guided runs
4. # against a fixed-model baseline. Raw artifacts are preserved and a readable
5. # summary is published to the Actions step summary.
6. #
7. # Required secrets (set in repo Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions):
8. #   COPILOT_CLI_TOKEN  – GitHub PAT with the `copilot` OAuth scope, used to
9. #                        authenticate the Copilot CLI process.
10. #   ANTHROPIC_API_KEY  – (Optional) needed if the harness calls Claude models
11. #                        directly rather than routing through Copilot.
12. #   OPENAI_API_KEY     – (Optional) needed if the harness calls OpenAI models
13. #                        directly.
14. #
15. # NOTE: Cost accounting is not tracked here. The harness may emit per-run token
16. # counts in its JSON output; treat those as estimates only.
17. 
18. name: Eval Harness
19. 
20. on:
21.   workflow_dispatch:
22.     inputs:
23.       suite:
24.         # Reserved for future task-filtering support (--task <id> flag).
25.         # Not currently passed to run-suite.mjs; all tasks run every time.
26.         description: "Test suite to run (informational; all tasks run)"
27.         required: false
28.         default: "all"
29.         type: choice
30.         options:
31.           - all
32.           - routing
33.           - quality
34.       fixed_model:
35.         # The model for fixed-run is configured in eval/config/models.json.
36.         # This input is kept for labeling the job name only; it is not currently
37.         # wired through the CLI. Update models.json to change the fixed model.
38.         description: "Model ID for fixed-model comparison run (label only)"
39.         required: false
40.         default: "claude-haiku-4.5"
41.       skip_fixed:
42.         description: "Skip the fixed-model comparison run"
43.         required: false
44.         default: "false"
45.         type: boolean
46. 
47.   # Weekly baseline on Mondays at 06:00 UTC; runs the full suite.
48.   schedule:
49.     - cron: "0 6 * * 1"
50. 
51. permissions:
52.   contents: read
53. 
54. defaults:
55.   run:
56.     shell: bash
57. 
58. # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
59. # Job: router-run  – run the eval suite with the model-router skill active
60. # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
61. jobs:
62.   router-run:
63.     name: "Eval – router mode"
64.     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
65.     steps:
66.       - name: Checkout
67.         uses: actions/checkout@v4
68. 
69.       - name: Set up Node.js
70.         uses: actions/setup-node@v4
71.         with:
72.           node-version: "22"
73.           cache: "npm"
74.           cache-dependency-path: "**/package-lock.json"
75. 
76.       - name: Install project dependencies
77.         # Only runs when a lockfile is present; the harness may be dependency-free.
78.         if: hashFiles('package-lock.json') != ''
79.         run: npm ci --ignore-scripts
80. 
81.       - name: Install Copilot CLI
82.         # The Copilot CLI is distributed as an npm package. Adjust the package
83.         # name / version if your harness uses a different distribution channel.
84.         # See eval/docs/ci-setup.md for alternatives (gh extension, direct download).
85.         run: npm install -g @github/copilot
86.         env:
87.           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
88. 
89.       - name: Run eval – router mode
90.         run: |
91.           mkdir -p eval/runs/router
92.           node eval/scripts/run-suite.mjs \
93.             --variant router \
94.             --suite-dir eval/runs/router
95.         env:
96.           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
97.           COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN }}
98.           ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
99.           OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
100.           # Surface run metadata in artifact JSON for later comparison.
101.           EVAL_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
102.           EVAL_REF: ${{ github.sha }}
103. 
104.       - name: Upload router-run artifacts
105.         uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
106.         if: always()
107.         with:
108.           name: eval-router-${{ github.run_id }}
109.           path: eval/runs/router/
110.           retention-days: 30
111. 
112.   # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
113.   # Job: fixed-run  – re-run the same suite pinned to a single model (baseline)
114.   # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
115.   fixed-run:
116.     name: "Eval – fixed model (${{ inputs.fixed_model || 'claude-haiku-4.5' }})"
117.     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
118.     # Skip when the caller explicitly opts out (manual runs only; schedule always runs).
119.     if: ${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' || inputs.skip_fixed != 'true' }}
120.     steps:
121.       - name: Checkout
122.         uses: actions/checkout@v4
123. 
124.       - name: Set up Node.js
125.         uses: actions/setup-node@v4
126.         with:
127.           node-version: "22"
128.           cache: "npm"
129.           cache-dependency-path: "**/package-lock.json"
130. 
131.       - name: Install project dependencies
132.         if: hashFiles('package-lock.json') != ''
133.         run: npm ci --ignore-scripts
134. 
135.       - name: Install Copilot CLI
136.         run: npm install -g @github/copilot
137.         env:
138.           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
139. 
140.       - name: Run eval – fixed model
141.         # Note: fixed_model input is not currently wired through the CLI; the
142.         # model is configured via eval/config/models.json. The input is kept for
143.         # reference and potential future use.
144.         run: |
145.           mkdir -p eval/runs/fixed
146.           node eval/scripts/run-suite.mjs \
147.             --variant fixed \
148.             --suite-dir eval/runs/fixed
149.         env:
150.           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
151.           COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN }}
152.           ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
153.           OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
154.           EVAL_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
155.           EVAL_REF: ${{ github.sha }}
156. 
157.       - name: Upload fixed-run artifacts
158.         uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
159.         if: always()
160.         with:
161.           name: eval-fixed-${{ github.run_id }}
162.           path: eval/runs/fixed/
163.           retention-days: 30
164. 
165.   # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
166.   # Job: summarize  – download both artifact sets, diff them, write step summary
167.   # ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
168.   summarize:
169.     name: "Publish eval summary"
170.     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
171.     needs: [router-run, fixed-run]
172.     # Run even if one job failed so we surface partial results.
173.     if: always()
174.     steps:
175.       - name: Checkout
176.         uses: actions/checkout@v4
177. 
178.       - name: Set up Node.js
179.         uses: actions/setup-node@v4
180.         with:
181.           node-version: "22"
182. 
183.       - name: Download router artifacts
184.         uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
185.         continue-on-error: true
186.         with:
187.           name: eval-router-${{ github.run_id }}
188.           path: eval/runs/router
189. 
190.       - name: Download fixed artifacts
191.         uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
192.         continue-on-error: true
193.         with:
194.           name: eval-fixed-${{ github.run_id }}
195.           path: eval/runs/fixed
196. 
197.       - name: Write step summary
198.         run: |
199.           node - <<'SUMMARY_EOF'
200.           const fs   = require('fs');
201.           const path = require('path');
202. 
203.           const STEP_SUMMARY = process.env.GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY;
204.           const lines = [];
205.           const push  = (s = '') => lines.push(s);
206. 
207.           const runId  = process.env.GITHUB_RUN_ID  || 'unknown';
208.           const ref    = process.env.GITHUB_SHA      || 'unknown';
209.           const repo   = process.env.GITHUB_REPOSITORY || 'unknown';
210.           const server = process.env.GITHUB_SERVER_URL || 'https://github.com';
211.           const artifactBase = `${server}/${repo}/actions/runs/${runId}`;
212. 
213.           push('# Eval Harness Results');
214.           push();
215.           push(`**Run:** [${runId}](${artifactBase})  `);
216.           push(`**Commit:** \`${ref.slice(0,8)}\`  `);
217.           push(`**Suite:** \`${process.env.EVAL_SUITE || 'all'}\``);
218.           push();
219. 
220.           function loadSummary(dir) {
221.             const summaryPath = path.join(dir, 'suite-summary.json');
222.             if (!fs.existsSync(summaryPath)) return null;
223.             try { return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(summaryPath, 'utf8')); }
224.             catch { return null; }
225.           }
226. 
227.           const router = loadSummary('eval/runs/router');
228.           const fixed  = loadSummary('eval/runs/fixed');
229. 
230.           if (!router && !fixed) {
231.             push('> ⚠️  No suite-summary.json found in either run directory.');
232.             push('> Check the individual job logs for errors.');
233.           } else {
234.             push('## Router vs Fixed-model Comparison');
235.             push();
236.             push('| Metric | Router | Fixed model |');
237.             push('|--------|--------|-------------|');
238. 
239.             const metrics = new Set([
240.               ...Object.keys(router || {}),
241.               ...Object.keys(fixed  || {}),
242.             ]);
243.             // Skip metadata fields
244.             const skip = new Set(['runId', 'ref', 'mode', 'model', 'suite', 'timestamp']);
245. 
246.             for (const key of metrics) {
247.               if (skip.has(key)) continue;
248.               const r = router?.[key] ?? '—';
249.               const f = fixed?.[key]  ?? '—';
250.               push(`| \`${key}\` | ${r} | ${f} |`);
251.             }
252.             push();
253. 
254.             if (router?.model) push(`> **Router selected models:** ${router.model}`);
255.             if (fixed?.model)  push(`> **Fixed model:** \`${fixed.model}\``);
256.             push();
257.           }
258. 
259.           push('## Artifacts');
260.           push();
261.           push(`Raw run files (JSON + logs) are attached to this workflow run:`);
262.           push(`- [eval-router-${runId}](${artifactBase}) — router-mode outputs`);
263.           push(`- [eval-fixed-${runId}](${artifactBase}) — fixed-model baseline`);
264.           push();
265.           push('Artifacts are retained for **30 days**.');
266.           push();
267.           push('## Caveats');
268.           push();
269.           push('- Token counts in suite-summary.json are estimates from the model API; they do not account for CLI overhead or retries.');
270.           push('- Cost comparisons are not included here. Map token counts to pricing manually using the model\'s current rate card.');
271.           push('- Scheduled runs use default inputs (`suite: all`, `fixed_model: claude-haiku-4.5`).');
272.           push('- The fixed-model run can be skipped on manual dispatch via the `skip_fixed` input.');
273. 
274.           fs.writeFileSync(STEP_SUMMARY, lines.join('\n') + '\n');
275.           console.log('Step summary written.');
276.           SUMMARY_EOF
277.         env:
278.           # GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY is set automatically by the runner; do not re-map it.
279.           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
280.           GITHUB_SHA: ${{ github.sha }}
281.           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}
282.           GITHUB_SERVER_URL: ${{ github.server_url }}
283.           EVAL_SUITE: ${{ inputs.suite || 'all' }}
284.

data.content:
Found two real bugs. Now implementing fixes:

**Bug 1** — `orderCandidates` uses `isSameModelFamily` (sub-family: haiku/sonnet/opus) rather than `getModelFamily` (provider-level: claude/gpt). This lets `claude-opus` and `claude-haiku` compete with gpt models in the first-priority slot when the impl model was `claude-sonnet`, bypassing true cross-provider diversity.

**Bug 2** — `buildAdditionalContext` only surfaces the fallback explanation when `selectedModelId === lastImplModel`, not when `selectedModelId === null` (getCurrent failed + all switches failed), silently dropping the hint.

Now I have a clear picture of the bugs. Let me implement the fixes:

**Bug 1**: `orderCandidates` uses `isSameModelFamily` (sub-family: haiku/sonnet/opus) instead of `getModelFamily` (high-level: claude/gpt) for diversity scoring. This means claude-haiku and claude-opus are treated as "different" from claude-sonnet and get score 0, competing with gpt models. They should get score 1 (same high-level family, different model).

**Bug 2**: `buildAdditionalContext` only shows the fallback explanation when `selectedModelId === lastImplModel`. 

There's also an edge case where if `selectedModelId` is null because both `getCurrent` and all the fallback switches failed, the fallback note gets dropped entirely—it should trigger in that scenario too. Now I'll implement these fixes and add regression tests to catch them.

   gpt-5.4 were unavailable.  Fix: switch to getModelFamily (provider-level:

   score 0 when the impl was claude (and vice-versa).

   selectedModelId === lastImplModel.  When getCurrent() fails and all
   switchTo calls also fail, selectedModelId is null — a clear degraded
   state — but the hint was silently dropped.  Fix: also emit the note when
   selectedModelId === null.

- orderCandidates: claude-opus/haiku deprioritised vs gpt when impl=claude-sonnet
- orderCandidates: claude deprioritised vs other-gpt when impl=gpt-5.1
- buildAdditionalContext: fallback note on selectedModelId === lastImplModel (explicit)
- buildAdditionalContext: fallback note on selectedModelId === null
- buildAdditionalContext: no fallback note when a distinct model was successfully selected

arguments.summary:
## Audit results & fixes

Two real bugs found and fixed in `policy.mjs`, with 5 regression tests added to `eval/routing-policy.test.mjs`. All 56 tests pass (51 pre-existing + 5 new). No doc changes needed — the user-visible routing contract ("prefer a different model for review than implementation") is unchanged.

---

### Bug 1 — `orderCandidates`: sub-family comparison used instead of provider-level family

**Root cause**: The sort scored diversity candidates using `isSameModelFamily`, which discriminates at the sub-family level (haiku / sonnet / opus). This gave `claude-opus` and `claude-haiku` the same score-0 (front-of-list) slot as gpt models when the last implementation used `claude-sonnet-4.6`. If `gpt-5` and `gpt-5.4` were both unavailable, the next candidate tried was `claude-opus-4.5` — still an Anthropic model, defeating the cross-provider diversity goal.

**Fix**: Replace `isSameModelFamily` with `getModelFamily` (which returns `"claude"` | `"gpt"`) in the scoring lambda inside `orderCandidates`. Now all claude models receive score 1 when the impl was any claude model, and all gpt models receive score 0 (and vice-versa). `isSameModelFamily` itself is untouched.

---

### Bug 2 — `buildAdditionalContext`: fallback note dropped when `selectedModelId === null`

**Root cause**: The fallback explanation ("A distinct review model was not available…") was only emitted when `decision.selectedModelId === lastImplModel`. When `getCurrent()` fails *and* all `switchTo` calls also fail, `selectedModelId` is `null` — a clearly degraded state — but the context hint was silently dropped, leaving the agent with no indication that diversity wasn't achieved.

**Fix**: Also emit the fallback line when `selectedModelId === null`.
